# Cognitive Core Individual Contributor License Agreement (ICLA)

**Version 1.0 — 2026-05-10**

Thank you for contributing to Cognitive Core. To keep the project legally clean
for every user — open-source and commercial — we ask each contributor to agree
to the terms below.

This ICLA is adapted from the [Apache Software Foundation ICLA][apache-icla], one
of the most widely accepted CLAs in the OSS world.

[apache-icla]: https://www.apache.org/licenses/icla.pdf

---

## How to sign

The easiest way: open any pull request. The [cla-assistant][cla-assistant] bot
will post a comment with a one-click sign-in link. You sign with your GitHub
account. That's it.

If you'd rather sign offline (e.g. on behalf of a company): email a signed copy
to **cla@cognitive-core.dev** and reference your GitHub username.

[cla-assistant]: https://cla-assistant.io/

---

## Agreement

By submitting a Contribution to this project, You accept and agree to the
following terms.

### 1. Definitions

- **You / Your** — the individual or legal entity making the Contribution.
- **Contribution** — any work of authorship submitted to the project, including
  code, documentation, designs, test data, and bug reports.
- **The Project** — the Cognitive Core project as hosted on github.com,
  including all its sub-repositories and successors.
- **Maintainer** — the legal entity or individual that controls the Project's
  copyright and trademark assignments. As of v1.0 this is the project's GitHub
  organization owner.

### 2. Grant of Copyright License

You hereby grant to the Maintainer and to recipients of software distributed by
the Maintainer a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free,
irrevocable copyright license to reproduce, prepare derivative works of,
publicly display, publicly perform, sublicense, and distribute Your Contributions
and such derivative works.

This is intentionally broad: it lets the Maintainer ship Your code under the MIT
license today and, if a Premium / commercial edition is ever released, under
that license too — without coming back to ask You.

### 3. Grant of Patent License

You hereby grant to the Maintainer and to recipients of software distributed by
the Maintainer a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, no-charge, royalty-free,
irrevocable (except as stated below) patent license to make, have made, use,
offer to sell, sell, import, and otherwise transfer the Project, where such
license applies only to those patent claims licensable by You that are
necessarily infringed by Your Contribution(s) alone or by combination of Your
Contribution(s) with the Project.

If any entity institutes patent litigation against the Project or You alleging
that Your Contribution constitutes direct or contributory patent infringement,
then any patent licenses granted to that entity under this Agreement for that
Contribution shall terminate as of the date such litigation is filed.

### 4. Representations

You represent that:

1. You are legally entitled to grant the above licenses.
2. Each of Your Contributions is Your original creation.
3. If Your employer(s) has rights to intellectual property You create that
   includes Your Contributions, You have received permission to make
   Contributions on behalf of that employer, OR Your employer has waived such
   rights for Your Contributions, OR Your employer has executed a separate
   Corporate CLA with the Maintainer.
4. Your Contribution does not contain code or content that, to the best of Your
   knowledge, infringes any third party's copyright, patent, trademark, or
   other intellectual property right.

### 5. Disclaimer

You provide Your Contributions on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied, including, without
limitation, any warranties or conditions of TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT,
MERCHANTABILITY, or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

### 6. Notification

You agree to notify the Maintainer of any facts or circumstances of which You
become aware that would make these representations inaccurate in any respect.

### 7. Governing Law

This Agreement is governed by the law of the jurisdiction where the Maintainer
is based. As of v1.0 this is the Russian Federation. Disputes go first to good-
faith negotiation, then to a mutually agreed arbitrator before any court.

---

## Quick FAQ

**Q: Why not just MIT for contributions?**
A: MIT is fine for users. For contributors, a CLA gives the Maintainer the
patent license needed to defend the project, and the relicensing flexibility
needed for a future Premium edition. The OSS edition stays MIT forever — see
the promise in `docs/PATENTS.md`.

**Q: Does this take my copyright?**
A: No. You keep copyright on Your work. You grant a license to use it.

**Q: I work for a company. What do I sign?**
A: For company-time contributions, ask your employer to sign the Corporate CLA
template (`CCLA.md` — coming soon). For personal-time contributions you can sign
this ICLA yourself, but talk to your employer first if there's any doubt.

**Q: What if my employer doesn't allow me to sign?**
A: Then please don't contribute proprietary work. We'd rather have a great bug
report than a lawsuit.

**Q: Can I revoke?**
A: The license grant is irrevocable for code already merged. You can stop
contributing at any time and we will not push you for new ones.

---

*If you signed an earlier version of this CLA, your sign-off remains valid.
Subsequent contributions are governed by the version current at the time you
made them.*
